get.description: Lists all DLP datasets configured for the account, including custom word lists and EDM datasets.

post.description: Creates a new DLP (Data Loss Prevention) dataset for storing custom detection patterns. Datasets can contain exact match data, word lists, or EDM (Exact Data Match) configurations.

case_sensitive.description:
Only applies to custom word lists.
Determines if the words should be matched in a case-sensitive manner
Cannot be set to false if `secret` is true or undefined

encoding_version.description:
Dataset encoding version

Non-secret custom word lists with no header are always version 1.
Secret EDM lists with no header are version 1.
Multicolumn CSV with headers are version 2.
Omitting this field provides the default value 0, which is interpreted
the same as 1.

secret.description:
Generate a secret dataset.

If true, the response will include a secret to use with the EDM encoder.
If false, the response has no secret and the dataset is uploaded in plaintext.

requestBody.description: Dataset description.

delete.description: This deletes all versions of the dataset.

put.description: Updates the configuration of an existing DLP dataset, such as its name, description, or detection settings.

case_sensitive.description:
Determines if the words should be matched in a case-sensitive manner.

Only required for custom word lists.

name.description: The name of the dataset, must be unique.

requestBody.description: Dataset description.

post.description: Creates a new version of a DLP dataset, allowing you to stage changes before activation. Used for single-column EDM and custom word lists.
post.summary: Prepare to upload a new version of a dataset

post.description:
This is used for single-column EDMv1 and Custom Word Lists. The EDM format
can only be created in the Cloudflare dashboard. For other clients, this
operation can only be used for non-secret Custom Word Lists. The body must
be a UTF-8 encoded, newline (NL or CRNL) separated list of words to be matched.

requestBody.description: Dataset. For custom wordlists this contains UTF-8 patterns separated by newline characters.

post.summary: Upload a new version of a dataset

post.description:
This is used for multi-column EDMv2 datasets. The EDMv2 format can only be
created in the Cloudflare dashboard. The columns in the response appear in
the same order as in the request.

requestBody.description: array of new columns to create for this dataset version.

post.summary: Sets the column information for a multi-column upload

post.description:
This is used for multi-column EDMv2 datasets. The EDMv2 format can only be
created in the Cloudflare dashboard.

requestBody.description: Dataset content.

post.summary: Upload a new version of a multi-column dataset

get.description: Lists all document fingerprints configured for DLP scanning in the account.
get.summary: Retrieve data about all document fingerprints.

post.description: Creates a new document fingerprint for DLP scanning. Document fingerprints detect documents that are structurally similar to the uploaded sample.

requestBody.description: Attributes of the new document fingerprint.

delete.description: Removes a document fingerprint from DLP configuration. Documents matching this fingerprint will no longer be detected.

get.summary: Retrieve data about a specific document fingerprint.

post.description: Updates metadata for an existing document fingerprint, such as its name or description.

requestBody.description: Attributes of the document fingerprint to update.

post.summary: Update the attributes of a single document fingerprint.

put.description: Uploads a new document to create or update a fingerprint. The document structure is analyzed to enable detection of similar documents.

requestBody.description: File used for document fingerprinting.

put.summary: Uploads a new version for a document fingerprint.

get.description: Retrieves the email provider mapping configuration for DLP email scanning.

200.description: Get Email Scanner Account Mapping response.

4XX.description: Get Email Scanner Account Mapping failure response.

post.description: Creates a mapping between a Cloudflare account and an email provider for DLP email scanning integration.

requestBody.description: Account mapping.

200.description: New Email Scanner Account Mapping response.

4XX.description: New Email Scanner Account Mapping failure response.

get.description: Lists all email scanner rules for an account.

200.description: List all email scanner rules response.

4XX.description: List all email scanner rules failure response.

patch.description: Reorders DLP email scanning rules by updating their priority values. Higher priority rules are evaluated first.

requestBody.description: Rule priorities.

200.description: Update Email Scanner Rule priorities response.

4XX.description: Update Email Scanner Rule priorities failure response.

post.description: Creates a new DLP email scanning rule that defines what content patterns to detect in email messages and what actions to take.

requestBody.description: Rule description.

4XX.description: New Email Scanner Rule failure response.

delete.description: Removes a DLP email scanning rule. The rule will no longer be applied to email messages.

4XX.description: Delete Email Scanner Rule failure response.

get.description: Gets detailed configuration for a specific DLP email scanning rule, including detection patterns and actions.

4XX.description: Get Email Scanner Rule failure response.

requestBody.description: Rule description.

4XX.description: Update Email Scanner Rule failure response.

get.description: Lists all DLP entries in an account.

requestBody.description: A new entry to create.

4XX.description: Create new custom entry failure response.

requestBody.description: Update to be applied to the entry.

post.description:
Integration entries can't be created, this will update an existing integration entry.
This is needed for our generated terraform API.

requestBody.description: This endpoint will update an existing integration entry. It is not possible to create new integration entries.

delete.description: This is a no-op as integration entires can't be deleted but is needed for our generated terraform API.

requestBody.description: Enable or disable integration entry in owning profile.

post.description:
Predefined entries can't be created, this will update an existing predefined entry.
This is needed for our generated terraform API.

requestBody.description: This endpoint will update an existing predefined entry. It is not possible to create new predefined entries.

delete.description: This is a no-op as predefined entires can't be deleted but is needed for our generated terraform API.

requestBody.description: Settings to enable or disable predefined entry in owning profile.

get.description: Fetches a DLP entry by ID.

requestBody.description: Update to be applied to the entry.

get.description: Retrieves current DLP usage limits and quotas for the account, including dataset limits and scan quotas.
get.summary: Fetch limits associated with DLP for account

post.description:
Validates whether this pattern is a valid regular expression. Rejects it if
the regular expression is too complex or can match an unbounded-length
string. The regex will be rejected if it uses `*` or `+`. Bound the maximum
number of characters that can be matched using a range, e.g. `{1,100}`.

requestBody.description: Validation query.

get.description: Gets the current payload logging configuration for DLP, showing whether matched content is being logged.

4XX.description: Failed to get payload log settings.

put.description: Enables or disables payload logging for DLP matches. When enabled, matched content is stored for review.

requestBody.description: New payload log settings.

4XX.description: Failed to set payload log settings.

get.description: Lists all DLP profiles in an account.

parameters.description: Return all profiles, including those that current account does not have access to.
parameters.name: all

get.description: Lists all DLP custom profiles in an account.

requestBody.description: A new profile to create.

get.description: Fetches a custom DLP profile by id.

requestBody.description: The updated parameters for the profile.

post.description: Creates a DLP predefined profile. Only supports enabling/disabling entries.

requestBody.description: Predefined profiles can not be created. This endpoint will only update an existing predefined profiles settings.

delete.description: This is a no-op as predefined profiles can't be deleted but is needed for our generated terraform API.

get.description: Fetches a predefined DLP profile by id.

put.description: Updates a DLP predefined profile. Only supports enabling/disabling entries.

requestBody.description: The updated parameters for the predefined profile.

get.description:
This is similar to `get_predefined` but only returns entries that are enabled.
This is needed for our terraform API
Fetches a predefined DLP profile by id.

post.description:
This is similar to `update_predefined` but only returns entries that are enabled.
This is needed for our terraform API
Creates a DLP predefined profile. Only supports enabling/disabling entries.

requestBody.description: Predefined profiles can not be created. This endpoint will only update an existing predefined profiles settings.

put.description:
This is similar to `update_predefined` but only returns entries that are enabled.
This is needed for our terraform API
Updates a DLP predefined profile. Only supports enabling/disabling entries.

requestBody.description: The updated parameters for the predefined profile.

get.description: Fetches a DLP profile by ID.

delete.summary: Delete (reset) DLP account-level settings to initial values.

patch.description: Missing fields keep their existing values.

requestBody.description: DLP settings fields to update.

put.description: Missing fields are reset to initial (unconfigured) values.

requestBody.description: New DLP settings.

dlp_NewPredefinedEntry.description:
Used to create a new predefined or integration entry.

Predefined or integration entries can not be updated via the API so
these fields will update the entry's settings.

profile_id.description:
This field is not used as the owning profile.
For predefined entries it is already set to a predefined profile.

data_classes.description: Data class IDs to associate with the profile. If omitted, existing associations are unchanged.

data_tags.description: Data tag IDs to associate with the profile. If omitted, existing associations are unchanged.

entries.description:
Custom entries from this profile.
If this field is omitted, entries owned by this profile will not be changed.

sensitivity_levels.description: Sensitivity levels to associate with the profile. If omitted, existing associations are unchanged.

shared_entries.description: Other entries, e.g. predefined or integration.

enabled_entries.description: Entries to enable for this predefined profile. Any entries not provided will be disabled.

entries.description: This field has been deprecated for `enabled_entries`.

id.description: The id of the predefined profile (uuid).

name.description: The name of the predefined profile.

open_access.description: Whether this profile can be accessed by anyone.

max_match_bytes.description:
Maximum number of bytes that the regular expression can match.

If this is `null` then there is no limit on the length. Patterns can use
`*` and `+`. Otherwise repeats should use a range `{m,n}` to restrict
patterns to the length. If this field is missing, then a default length
limit is used.

Note that the length is specified in bytes. Since regular expressions
use UTF-8 the pattern `.` can match up to 4 bytes. Hence `.{1,256}`
has a maximum length of 1024 bytes.

dlp_UpdateEmailRulePriorities.description:
Used to update multiple email rule priorities as an atomic action,
to support patterns such as swapping the priorities of two email rules.

encoding_version.description: Encoding version to use for dataset.

secret.description:
The secret to use for Exact Data Match datasets.

This is not present in Custom Wordlists.

version.description: The version to use when uploading the dataset.

ai_context_analysis.description: Whether AI context analysis is enabled at the account level.

ocr.description: Whether OCR is enabled at the account level.

allowed_match_count.description: Related DLP policies will trigger when the match count exceeds the number set.

data_classes.description: Data classes associated with this profile.

data_tags.description: Data tags associated with this profile.

sensitivity_levels.description: Sensitivity levels associated with this profile.

updated_at.description: When the profile was lasted updated.

allOf.description:
Masking level for payload logs.

- `full`: The entire payload is masked.
- `partial`: Only partial payload content is masked.
- `clear`: No masking is applied to the payload content.
- `default`: DLP uses its default masking behavior.

public_key.description: Base64-encoded public key for encrypting payload logs. Null when payload logging is disabled.

dlp_ContextAwareness.description: Scan the context of predefined entries to only return matches surrounded by keywords.

enabled.description: If true, scan the context of predefined entries to only return matches surrounded by keywords.

dlp_PayloadLogSettingUpdateLegacy.description: Request model for the legacy payload log settings endpoint.

allOf.description:
Masking level for payload logs.

- `full`: The entire payload is masked.
- `partial`: Only partial payload content is masked.
- `clear`: No masking is applied to the payload content.
- `default`: DLP uses its default masking behavior.

public_key.description:
Base64-encoded public key for encrypting payload logs.

- Set to null or empty string to disable payload logging.
- Set to a non-empty base64 string to enable payload logging with the given key.

For customers with configurable payload masking feature rolled out:
- If the field is missing, the existing setting will be kept. Note that this is different from setting to null or empty string.

For all other customers:
- If the field is missing, the existing setting will be cleared.

dlp_SkipConfig.description: Content types to exclude from context analysis and return all matches.

files.description: If the content type is a file, skip context analysis and return all matches.

dlp_DlpSettingsUpdate.description:
Request model for updating DLP account-level settings.
All fields are optional. Missing fields behave differently for PUT vs PATCH:
- PUT: missing fields reset to initial (unconfigured) values.
- PATCH: missing fields keep existing values.

ai_context_analysis.description: Whether AI context analysis is enabled at the account level.

ocr.description: Whether OCR is enabled at the account level.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

allowed_match_count.description: Related DLP policies will trigger when the match count exceeds the number set.

data_classes.description: Data class IDs to associate with the profile.

data_tags.description: Data tag IDs to associate with the profile.

sensitivity_levels.description: Sensitivity levels to associate with the profile.

shared_entries.description: Entries from other profiles (e.g. pre-defined Cloudflare profiles, or your Microsoft Information Protection profiles).

updated_at.description:
Stores when the dataset was last updated.

This includes name or description changes as well as uploads.

dlp_SensitivityLevelRef.description: A reference pairing a sensitivity group with a specific level within that group.

ai_context_available.description: Indicates whether this entry has AI remote service validation.

available.description: Indicates whether this entry has any form of validation that is not an AI remote service.

id.description: The id of the predefined profile (uuid).

name.description: The name of the predefined profile.

open_access.description: Whether this profile can be accessed by anyone.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

dlp_EntryProfile.description: Computed entry field for a profile that an entry is shared into.

dlp_PayloadLogSettingUpdate.description:
Request model for payload log settings within the DLP settings endpoint.
Unlike the legacy endpoint, null and missing are treated identically here
(both mean "not provided" for PATCH, "reset to default" for PUT).

allOf.description:
Masking level for payload logs.

- `full`: The entire payload is masked.
- `partial`: Only partial payload content is masked.
- `clear`: No masking is applied to the payload content.
- `default`: DLP uses its default masking behavior.

public_key.description:
Base64-encoded public key for encrypting payload logs.

- Set to a non-empty base64 string to enable payload logging with the given key.
- Set to an empty string to disable payload logging.
- Omit or set to null to leave unchanged (PATCH) or reset to disabled (PUT).
